items.title: Ridge vs Lasso constraint geometry
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  ols["OLS: min squared error"] --> ridge["Ridge: error + L2 ball"]
  ols --> lasso["Lasso: error + L1 diamond"]
  ridge --> shrink["all coefs shrink toward 0"]
  lasso --> sparse["some coefs hit exact 0"]
items.caption: Both methods constrain coefficient size, but L2's round constraint shrinks smoothly while L1's corners encourage sparsity.

items.title: Ridge stabilizes XᵀX inversion
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  xtx["X transpose X"] --> ill["near singular if collinear"]
  ill --> fix["add lambda I"]
  fix --> stable["invertible, stable beta"]
items.caption: Adding λI to XᵀX improves numerical conditioning when predictors are correlated.
